# workflow-gen specification

`workflow-gen` generates GitHub Actions workflows from source-embedded YAML
fragments.

## Marker format

Fragments are delimited with `//<workflow-gen>` and
`//</workflow-gen>`. Shell-style `#` prefixes are also accepted.

```ts
//<workflow-gen>
// name: my-workflow
// required: true
// on:
//   push:
//     branches: [main]
//   pull_request: {}
// jobs:
//   build:
//     name: Build
//     steps:
//       - uses: actions/checkout@v5
//       - uses: ./.github/actions/setup
//       - run: bun run build
//</workflow-gen>
```

Top-level fields:

- `name` (required): output filename stem and merge key.
- `required` (optional): when `true`, job display names are written to
  `required-workflows.json`. This key is stripped from the emitted workflow.
- All other keys are GitHub Actions workflow YAML.

Template variables are expanded before YAML parsing:

- `$$file`: relative source file path from the base directory.
- `$$directory`: relative source file directory from the base directory.

## Merge rules

Fragments with the same `name` are merged in deterministic source-path order:

- `on`: union of trigger definitions.
- `env` and `permissions`: shallow merge, later values win.
- `concurrency`: last writer wins.
- `jobs`: merge by job id; repeated `steps` arrays are concatenated.
- other keys: last writer wins, with verbose conflict warnings.

Jobs without `runs-on` and without `uses:` get `runs-on: ubuntu-latest`.

## Output

Generated workflows are written to `.github/workflows/<name>.yml` with:

```yaml
# AUTO-GENERATED by workflow-gen -- DO NOT EDIT
# Source: path/to/source.ts
```

The generator refuses to overwrite an existing workflow file that lacks the
generated header. `check` reports missing, changed, and stale generated files.

`required-workflows.json` is written at the repo root and contains sorted job
display names from workflows marked `required: true`.

Required workflows must include an unfiltered `pull_request` or
`pull_request_target` trigger so branch protection checks run on every PR.

## CLI

```bash
bun run workflow-gen:generate
bun run workflow-gen:check
bun run workflow-gen:e2e-test
```

Options:

- `--base <path>`: base folder to scan (default: git root).
- `--output <path>`: workflow output folder (default:
  `<base>/.github/workflows`).
- `--verbose`: print scan and merge diagnostics.
